name: tobe_sql_generation
objective: Generate one executable Oracle/MyBatis TO-BE SQL statement that preserves the source result set while following TO-BE schema mappings.
rules: Generate or fix every SQL statement for Oracle 19c syntax.
mapping_schema_text contains [MIGRATION_MAPPING_RULES] from NEXT_MIG_INFO/NEXT_MIG_INFO_DTL. Treat these rules as the primary source of table and column mapping truth.
mapping_schema_text also contains SQL conversion RAG from NEXT_MIG_RAG_INFO where CATEGORY='SQL_CONVERSION' and USE_YN='Y'.
[SQL_CONVERSION_GENERAL_RAG_GUIDANCE] contains mandatory guidance where RULE_TYPE='GENERAL' and SOURCE_TABLES matches the current source table list or is empty.
[SQL_CONVERSION_SEARCH_RAG_TOP_K_BY_SQL_BLOCK] is a JSON array searched per SQL block where RULE_TYPE='SEARCH'. Each item has matched_block_sql, guidance, source_sql, and target_sql.
source_sql and target_sql inside [SQL_CONVERSION_SEARCH_RAG_TOP_K_BY_SQL_BLOCK] are SQL conversion examples, not SQL tuning examples.
Use SQL_CONVERSION RAG as conversion pattern guidance only. Do not copy an example when it conflicts with MIGRATION_MAPPING_RULES, the current from_sql, target_schema, or last_error.
Preserve the original query structure, filters, aggregation, joins, aliases, MyBatis dynamic tags, and bind parameter names whenever possible.
Do not remove or replace MyBatis bind markers such as #{param} or ${param}. Keep the original parameter names and marker style.
Keep existing MyBatis dynamic tags such as <if>, <choose>, <when>, <otherwise>, <where>, <trim>, and <foreach>. Only change SQL expressions, table names, and column names required by mapping rules.
Every physical TO-BE table in the output SQL must use target_schema.TABLE_NAME format.
If mapping_schema_text already includes a schema on TO_TABLE, remove that existing schema and apply target_schema instead.
Do not add target_schema to DUAL, CTE names, inline view aliases, table aliases, or subquery aliases.
Do not split dynamic SQL into multiple separate SQL statements. Preserve it as one Oracle/MyBatis SQL template.
Return only one executable Oracle/MyBatis SQL template. Do not include explanations, markdown, JSON, PL/SQL blocks, multiple SQL statements, or a trailing semicolon.
correct_sql_hint_json is a JSON array of up to 2 previously verified TOBE_CORRECT_SQL examples searched from similar FR SQL. Use them only as hints.
When correct_sql_hint_json conflicts with current from_sql, mapping_schema_text, target_schema, or last_error, current inputs take priority.
user_instruction: Apply the rules in priority order and return exactly one Oracle/MyBatis TO-BE SQL template.
